wpcs_compliance.analysis: Clean constructor with proper tab indentation, snake_case variable names. Missing explicit public access modifier, acceptable in PHP 7+ but less explicit. Correct get_option() usage.

sql_safety.analysis: Uses get_option() without direct SQL. WordPress option API handles parameterization internally.

security.analysis: Reads plugin options via get_option() with safe boolean coercion. Handles both boolean true and string 'true' from option storage. No user-controlled input processed.

performance.analysis: Constructor triggers chunk processing immediately — expensive on every instantiation. fetch_and_check_homepage_resources() called unconditionally may spawn HTTP requests. Should defer to a setup method.

wp_api_usage.analysis: get_option() used correctly with proper default (false). Idiomatic WordPress option retrieval pattern. empty() check is standard.

code_quality.analysis: Constructor performing I/O operations (HTTP requests via fetch_and_check_homepage_resources) makes unit testing difficult. I/O in constructors violates single-responsibility principle.

accessibility.analysis: Backend initialization with no HTML output. No accessibility concerns.

code:
/**
     * Get all the settings for this plugin for @see woocommerce_admin_fields() function.
     *
     * @return array Array of settings for @see woocommerce_admin_fields() function.
     */
function get_settings()
        {
        $settings = array(
            'section_title' => array(
                'name' => __('Round Up Prices', 'woocommerce-settings-tab-wrup') ,
                'type' => 'title',
                'desc' => 'This setting page allows you to round the prices in the Woocommerce product catalog in order to force the visualy appealing values instead of ugly decimals.',
                'woocommerce-settings-tab-wrup',
                'id' => 'wc_settings_tab_wrup_section_title'
            ) ,
            'button' => array(
                "name" => "Please, choose the rounding method",
                "id" => "wrup_roundup_setting",
                "type" => "radio",
                "desc" => "",
                "options" => array(
                    "disabled" => "Disabled",
                    "10" => ".10",
                    "15" => ".15",
                    "25" => ".25",
                    "50" => ".50",
                    "100" => "1.00"
                ) ,
                "parent" => "woocommerce-round-up-prices",
                "std" => "right"
            ) ,
            'section_end' => array(
                'type' => 'sectionend',
                'id' => 'wc_settings_tab_wrup_section_end'
            )
        );
        return apply_filters('wc_settings_tab_wrup_settings', $settings);
        }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Good WPCS: snake_case, correct in_array() with strict third argument, sanitize_text_field() applied. Spacing around operators is consistent. Minor: WP_CLI::error() should have explicit return.

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL queries. Uses get_site_option() which is safe. No database writes.

security.analysis: sanitize_text_field() applied to $args[0] before use in output and before passing to devmode_toggle(). Strict in_array() validation with $valid_modes prevents unexpected values.

performance.analysis: Single get_site_option() read and O(1) in_array() check. WP-CLI admin tool context makes performance overhead acceptable.

wp_api_usage.analysis: get_site_option() for multisite-aware option. sanitize_text_field() on CLI input. __() consistently for translatable strings. sprintf() correctly used with translations.

code_quality.analysis: Clean three-branch dispatch: no args (status), invalid args (error), valid (execute). Strict in_array() check prevents injection. self::$devmode fallback is good defensive programming.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Depends on VarnishPurger::$devmode static and VarnishDebug::devmode_toggle() — internal plugin class methods without runtime guards.

i18n.analysis: All user-visible strings use __() with correct text domain. Translator comments present. sprintf() used correctly with translated format strings.

accessibility.analysis: WP-CLI command with no HTML output. WP_CLI::log/error/success provide appropriate semantic feedback.

reasoning.key_observation: Well-structured WP-CLI command applying sanitize_text_field() to user input and strict in_array() validation.
